# Bankruptcy Cost -- How to Reduce What You Pay [2026 Guide]
> Complete breakdown of bankruptcy costs in 2026 -- filing fees, attorney fees, credit counseling, and hidden costs. Compare Chapter 7 vs....

## About
bankruptcycost.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Bankruptcy Attorney Fees by State -- National Averages -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/attorney-fees-by-state.html
- Bankruptcy Attorney Fees: What to Expect - bankruptcycost.org -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/attorney-fees.html
- The Cost of NOT Filing Bankruptcy -- When Inaction Is More Expensive -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/cost-of-doing-nothing.html
- The Cost of NOT Filing Bankruptcy - bankruptcycost.org -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/cost-of-not-filing.html
- Can I File Bankruptcy for Free? Fee Waivers and Installment Plans - bankruptcycost.org -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/fee-waiver.html
- Bankruptcy Filing Fee Waiver -- Income Requirements and Form 103B -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/filing-fee-waiver.html
- Bankruptcy Filing Fees by Chapter (2024-2026) - bankruptcycost.org -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/filing-fees.html
- Hidden Costs of Bankruptcy You Should Know About - bankruptcycost.org -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/hidden-costs.html
- Bankruptcy Cost -- How to Reduce What You Pay: https://bankruptcycost.org/
- Chapter 7 Bankruptcy Cost -- Filing Fees, Attorney Fees & Total Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/chapter-7-cost.html
- Chapter 13 Bankruptcy Cost -- Attorney Fees, Filing Fees & Plan Payments: https://bankruptcycost.org/chapter-13-cost.html
- Average Cost of Bankruptcy by State -- Attorney Fees & Filing Costs [2026] -- Bankruptcy Cost: https://bankruptcycost.org/average-cost-by-state.html

- Website: https://openbankruptcyproject.org

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
